name: ExtensionDetectedIssue_Category
title: R5: Type of detected issue, e.g. drug-drug, duplicate therapy, etc (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `DetectedIssue.category` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`DetectedIssue.category` 0..* `CodeableConcept`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `DetectedIssue.category` 0..* `CodeableConcept`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `DetectedIssue.category` has a context of DetectedIssue based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `DetectedIssue`.
Element `DetectedIssue.category` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Type of detected issue, e.g. drug-drug, duplicate therapy, etc (new)
element.comment:
Element `DetectedIssue.category` has a context of DetectedIssue based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `DetectedIssue`.
Element `DetectedIssue.category` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
In addition to the required category valueset, this element allows various categorization schemes based on the owner’s definition of the category and effectively multiple categories can be used at once.  The level of granularity is defined by the category concepts in the value set.

element.short: Type of detected issue, e.g. drug-drug, duplicate therapy, etc
element.definition: A code that classifies the general type of detected issue.
element.comment: In addition to the required category valueset, this element allows various categorization schemes based on the owner’s definition of the category and effectively multiple categories can be used at once.  The level of granularity is defined by the category concepts in the value set.

binding.description: Codes for high level detected issue categories.
